body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772524049842419) ## Action Performed: 1. In New Expensify (NewDot), go to Reports. 2. Apply the following filters: Type: Expense Report, Status: Draft, Outstanding, Approved, Date: After December 31, 2025, From: Specific user 3. Observe the results list shows “Nothing to show.” 4. Change Type from Expense Report → Expense (keep all other filters the same) 5. Observe that expenses now appear in the list 6. In Classic (OldDot), go to Reports and apply equivalent filters: Date starting 2025-12-31, Statuses Draft / Outstanding / Approved, Same user 7. Observe that two matching Expense Reports appear. ## Expected Result: New Expensify should return the same two Expense Reports as Classic when the same filters are applied. ## Actual Result: New Expensify returns no results (“Nothing to show”) when Type = Expense Report is selected. Classic returns 2 matching reports with equivalent filters. Switching Type to Expense in New immediately shows results, indicating the issue is specific to Expense Report filtering. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772524049842419) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Reduce forced focus mode switching

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Melvin Triage
labels.description: Automatic AI Analysis of an issue

body:
Slack Thread Context: https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1771328820064029?thread_ts=1771328820.064029&cid=C05LX9D6E07 **What the problem is:** Users are being automatically switched into `#focus mode` (an unread-only filter) in a way that feels jarring and confusing, causing chats to “disappear” from the Inbox during normal navigation. **What the expected output is:** - Reduce accidental/over-eager auto-switching by basing the threshold on the number of reports visible in the user’s LHN (not total reports in Onyx), so fewer users are forced into the mode. **Debugging info:** - Reference: https://fsty.io/v/Cw5YnkdF - Current logic described: auto-switch triggers when a user has **20+ reports in Onyx**, rather than **20+ reports in LHN**. - Example mentioned: user had **6 reports in LHN** but still got switched. - User impact reported: confusion that chats disappear after clicking into them and then navigating elsewhere; consistent negative feedback. **Potential solutions or implementation notes (if applicable):** - First step agreed: change threshold calculation to use **LHN report count** instead of **Onyx report count**.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ShridharGoel
Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022028892030546284710
  • Upwork Job ID: 2028892030546284710
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-03-03

Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Create an expense. 4. Open the report. 5. Click Submit. 6. Click description, enter applausetester+ndk80@applause.expensifail.com and save it. 7. Rename report to APPROVE_ERROR. 8. Click Submit. 9. Click Approve. 10. Right click on "failed to submit the report. Add at least one approver as an expense description" system message > Copy to clipboard. 11. Paste the content. 12. Right click on "failed to approve. This report cannot be approved" system message > Copy to clipboard. 13. Paste the content. ## Expected Result: The copied content will be the same as the system message. ## Actual Result: The copied content is different from the system message. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12813631-76ae-4ba0-a677-0c1180f0ad4f [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.28-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** N/A - new feature, doesn't exist in prod **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/74967 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** isodjnosidjiojiojiso@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Enable distance rates. - Change distance unit to Kilometers. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to self DM. 3. Click + > Track distance > Manual. 4. Enter amount > Next. 5. Click Create expense. 6. Open the expense. 7. Click Distance field. ## Expected Result: In Step 3 and 4, when creating distance expense, distance unit should default to kilometer (from the changes in precondition). ## Actual Result: In Step 3 and 4, when creating distance expense, old distance unit (mile) persists. In Step 6, after creating the expense, the distance amount persists but with the different unit (kilometer). In Step 7, distance editor still shows miles when the menu item shows kilometers. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65fa4757-a7b0-4614-bf99-b245655385ed [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Prerequisite 2: At least one rate created different than default. Prerequisite 3: Account has Self DM. 1. Open the staging.new.expensify.com 2. Navigate to Self DM. 3. Create a map distance expense with a rate different than default. 4. Open the report of the just created expense. 5. Tap on "Report" field and select "Create Report" 6. Navigate to workspace chat and reopen report. 7. Note that distance rate and unit weren´t changed. 8. Tap on "Report" field again and select "Remove from report" 9. Navigate to Self DM and open report again. 10. Note that distance rate and unit were changed. ## Expected Result: Distance rate and unit should remain the same after moving a distanc expense to Self DM again. ## Actual Result: Distance rate and unit are changed after a distance track expense is moved to Self DM again. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f615b6a-9940-4f07-9969-6996edd894b9 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.28-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** N/A - new feature, doesn't exist in prod **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/74967 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** sjiondsjndosijdonijso@gmaal.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Enable Taxes and Distance rates. - Add two tax rates - 5% and 10 %. - Add a new distance rates - 1.00 / mile. - Enable Track tax in Distance rates settings. - Assign 5% tax rate to 0.725 / mile (default rate) with any tax reclaimable amount. - Assign 10% tax rate to 1.00 / mile with any tax reclaimable amount. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to self DM. 3. Create a manual distance expense with the default distance rate (0,725 / mile). 4. Click Submit it to someone. 5. Select workspace chat. 6. On confirm page, click Rate. 7. Select another rate (1.00 / mile). 8. Click Create expense. ## Expected Result: Expense will be created without issue. ## Actual Result: "Tax no longer valid" is shown on Tax field when it cannot be edited. User is blocked from submitting the distance expense. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57403c86-73c5-47b9-9310-bd2f8d00ff6e [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
